content: The application of the $S=(C, \pi, T, R)$ framework and Progressive Disclosure mechanic has fundamentally reshaped the scope of Epics 109-112, significantly reducing the need for complex AI reasoning in areas like routing and emphasizing deterministic boundaries. This shift prevents agent hallucination and ensures reliable execution.
one_liner: The new Skill Standard ($S=(C, \pi, T, R)$) is driving a major architectural refactor, shrinking AI complexity and boosting deterministic execution.
source_context: By applying the $S=(C, \pi, T, R)$ framework and the Progressive Disclosure mechanic, the scope and nature of Epics 109–112 fundamentally change.

content: Agents must strictly adhere to the formal $S=(C, \pi, T, R)$ skill boundaries to prevent Agent Sprawl and Ghost Actions. They are prohibited from creating flat markdown workflows relying on LLM intuition. Before creating or editing any skill, agents must execute a `view_file` on `SKILL_TEMPLATE.md` to retrieve the explicit JSON/YAML contract structure.
one_liner: Agents must strictly follow the $S=(C, \pi, T, R)$ skill standard and consult `SKILL_TEMPLATE.md` before any skill creation or editing.
source_context: ## Skill Architecture Law ... Prohibited: Creating flat markdown workflows relying on LLM intuition. Mandatory: ALL new and refactored skills must meticulously implement the YAML frontmatter and Progressive Disclosure architecture defined centrally. Initialization: Before creating or editing ANY skill, you MUST execute a `view_file` on `D:\GitHub\global_agent\.agent\skills\SKILL_TEMPLATE.md` to retrieve the explicit JSON/YAML contract structure. Failure to do so violates the deterministic system boundary.

content: For mechanical engineering tasks, the AI's role is redefined from performing calculations to orchestrating deterministic "Level 3 Deterministic Scripts." This approach, exemplified by the `FMEA_Compliance_Checker.py` script, ensures accuracy and prevents the agent from hallucinating CAD structures or engineering tolerances.
one_liner: AI's role in mechanical tasks is redefined as an orchestrator for deterministic scripts, preventing hallucination in critical calculations.
source_context: The AI doesn't *do* the math; it acts as the orchestrator. These tickets must be rewritten as 'Level 3 Deterministic Scripts.' ... This stops the agent from hallucinating CAD structures.

content: The Instrumentation Dashboard is being refactored from a passive UI into an active "Trace Evaluator." Its primary function will be to parse the FastMCP JSONL stream and provide cryptographic proof that sub-tools were executed, thereby preventing "Ghost Actions" where agents claim execution without actual completion.
one_liner: The Instrumentation Dashboard is evolving into a "Trace Evaluator" to cryptographically verify tool execution and prevent "Ghost Actions."
source_context: To stop 'Ghost Actions,' the dashboard cannot just be a passive UI. It must be the 'Trace Evaluator' that verifies a skill actually executed its required tools before returning Success ($T$).

content: A new Epic, "The Great Migration," is required to address significant technical debt. This involves converting over 40 legacy linear `.agent/workflows/` from unstructured text into structured `.agent/skills/` directories, adhering to the new S-Standard with YAML metadata.
one_liner: A "Great Migration" Epic is launched to convert 40+ legacy workflows to the new S-Standard, addressing critical technical debt.
source_context: Before we can safely execute any of the above, we have 40+ legacy linear `.agent/workflows/` sitting as unstructured text. ... This is the technical debt paydown phase...

content: EN-OS is highly mature in "Living Content Assets" and "Creative Asset Generation." The `ast-patcher` tool effectively handles the "Update" phase for surgically altering Astro components, and existing LinkedIn workflows serve as a multi-node creative pipeline. The primary gap is the "Monitor" and "Diff" scheduled loops, which are now slated for development.
one_liner: EN-OS is highly mature in living content management with `ast-patcher` and LinkedIn workflows, needing only "Monitor" and "Diff" loops to complete the Three-Agent Architecture.
source_context: EN-OS Current State: **Highly Mature.** ... You already possess the `ast-patcher` tool which perfectly fulfills the 'Update' phase... your `draft_linkedin_post.md`, `publish_post.md`, and `harvest_linkedin.md` workflows act as the multi-node creative pipeline.

content: The $S=(C, \pi, T, R)$ framework significantly enhances routing efficiency by allowing the orchestrator to load only Level 1 YAML metadata (~50 tokens per skill) for hundreds of skills without degrading AI reasoning. It also enables deterministic evaluation by scanning Local MCP traces to verify tool execution, instantly catching "Ghost Actions" where agents hallucinate outcomes.
one_liner: The $S=(C, \pi, T, R)$ standard boosts routing efficiency and eliminates "Ghost Actions" through metadata-driven loading and deterministic trace evaluation.
source_context: Routing Efficiency: When EN-OS boots, the orchestrator only reads the YAML blocks of all skills (Level 1). This consumes ~50 tokens per skill, allowing us to load 100+ skills into memory simultaneously... Deterministic Evaluation (Stopping Ghosts): ...our external evaluation loops can scan the Local MCP trace. If the trace proves that the tool *run_command* on `python scripts/...` did not occur, we know the agent hallucinated the BOM, instantly catching Ghost Actions.
